skill.name: UI Visual Validator
skill.description: Validates UI components and layouts by analyzing visual structures, checking element relationships, and verifying design consistency across screenshots or mockups.
skill.summary: AI-powered visual validation for UI components and layouts
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found no executable code, no suspicious patterns, and no risk factors. This is a prompt-only skill that generates content descriptions. Risk score is 0/100. The skill operates purely in the AI content generation domain with no system-level access.

content.value_statement: Manual UI validation is time-consuming and prone to human error. This skill uses AI to automatically analyze visual structures, verify element relationships, and ensure design consistency across components.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes UI component structures and identifies visual hierarchies
Verifies element relationships and spacing consistency
Checks design token usage across components
Identifies potential layout issues and alignment problems
limitations: Cannot process actual image files directly - works with text descriptions
Relies on accurate prompt input for best results
Cannot execute code or run visual tests
Analysis is based on structural descriptions, not pixel-perfect validation

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Validation
use_cases.description: Frontend developers use this skill to validate their component implementations match design specifications by analyzing structure descriptions.

use_cases.title: Design System Auditors
use_cases.description: Design system maintainers verify component consistency across libraries by checking structural patterns and design token usage.

use_cases.title: QA Visual Testing
use_cases.description: QA engineers validate UI changes by comparing component descriptions and identifying unexpected visual differences.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this UI component description and validate its structure: [INSERT COMPONENT DESCRIPTION]. Check for proper hierarchy, spacing, and element relationships.
prompt_templates.scenario: Validating a single component against design rules

prompt_templates.title: Design Token Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these component styles [INSERT STYLES] and verify they follow our design system tokens. List any deviations found.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking component styles against design system

prompt_templates.title: Layout Consistency Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two component descriptions [INSERT DESCRIPTION 1] and [INSERT DESCRIPTION 2]. Identify any layout or spacing inconsistencies.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility Structure Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this component structure [INSERT DESCRIPTION] for proper heading hierarchy, landmark usage, and semantic element placement. Suggest improvements.

output_examples.input: Validate this button component: Large primary button with 16px padding, rounded corners (8px), blue background (#0066FF), white text, centered label.
output_examples.output:
Validation Results:

✓ Proper padding (16px) for interactive element
✓ Consistent border-radius (8px)
✓ Valid primary color usage
✓ Centered text alignment

Recommendations:
- Consider adding hover state contrast check
- Verify focus ring visibility for accessibility

output_examples.input: Compare card component A (header: 24px, body: 16px, footer: 16px) vs card B (header: 24px, body: 16px, footer: 24px).
output_examples.output:
Inconsistency Found:

Card A footer: 16px padding
Card B footer: 24px padding

This creates visual inconsistency between card components. Recommend standardizing footer padding to 16px across all card variants.

best_practices: Provide detailed component descriptions including all relevant style properties and structural elements for accurate validation
Use consistent naming conventions when describing components to improve analysis accuracy
Include context about the design system or style guide being used for validation
anti_patterns: Providing vague descriptions like 'a nice button' without specific properties or measurements
Skipping design system context - always reference the standards being validated against
Ignoring the validation results - use the output to actually improve component quality

faq.question: Can this skill validate actual image files?
faq.answer: No, this skill works with text descriptions of UI components, not actual image files. For image validation, use dedicated screenshot comparison tools.

faq.question: What design systems are supported?
faq.answer: The skill can validate against any design system described in the prompt. Provide your design tokens and rules for best results.

faq.question: Does this replace visual regression testing?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides structural and design token validation, not pixel-perfect comparison. Use it alongside visual regression testing tools.

faq.question: Can it validate accessibility?
faq.answer: It can check for structural accessibility patterns like heading hierarchy and semantic elements, but not full accessibility compliance.

faq.question: How accurate is the validation?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on the detail in your component descriptions. More detailed inputs yield more accurate validation results.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for automated testing pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, you can integrate the skill outputs into CI/CD pipelines by capturing validation results as part of your component review process.
